positive_description: Extremely subtle deformation. At +3.0, no consistent visual change from neutral is discernible in frontal or 3/4 views. If an asymmetric pair member (psi22/psi23), it would be the left-lateralized component of a fifth conjugate pair.
negative_description: Extremely subtle deformation. At -3.0, visually indistinguishable from neutral.
symmetry_notes: Indeterminate. If the conjugate pair pattern continues beyond psi20 (pair at 22/23 after symmetric psi21), this would be asymmetric. Cannot be confirmed from renders — the deformation is below visual threshold.
artifact_notes: No mesh artifacts at +/-3.0. Safe well beyond tested range.
useful_for: micro-texture in expression blends at very high weights
potentially part of fifth conjugate pair with psi23

interactions.psi23: Predicted conjugate pair if the alternating symmetric/asymmetric pattern continues

prose: Psi22 sits deep in the mid-frequency expression PCA band. All seven renders appear visually identical to the neutral face at the tested weight range (-3.0 to +3.0). No feature change — mouth, eyes, brows, cheeks, jaw — is discernible across the sweep or between the 3/4 profile views. If the alternating pattern from psi10-20 continues (symmetric modulator, then asymmetric pair, repeat), psi22 would be the first member of a fifth conjugate pair. However, this deep into the PCA ordering, components increasingly reflect training-subject idiosyncrasies, and the clean architectural patterns may not persist.
